title: Podman install for Ubuntu 18.04: slirp4netns not found
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**

Hi all.

I'm trying to install podman on ubuntu 18.04.

I'm following the guide here: https://podman.io/getting-started/installation
```
. /etc/os-release
echo "deb https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_${VERSION_ID}/ /" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.list
curl -L https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_${VERSION_ID}/Release.key | sudo apt-key add -
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get -y upgrade
sudo apt-get -y install podman
```
When I try to start a container I am met with this error message from systemd
```
level=error msg="could not find slirp4netns, the network namespace won't be configured
```
Manually running the container 
```
ERRO[0009] could not find slirp4netns, the network namespace won't be configured: exec: "slirp4netns": executable file not found in $PATH 
```

Despite this error message the containers are still ran by the user and seem to be working.
But this error worries me if this to be used in a production environment,

When I look into the opensuse repo is **no** reference to slirp4netns

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. install podman on ubuntu 18.04 from https://podman.io/getting-started/installation

2. run a container as a non root user

3. See error messages in console

**Describe the results you received:**
```
ERRO[0009] could not find slirp4netns, the network namespace won't be configured: exec: "slirp4netns": executable file not found in $PATH 
9460790aa6707e5feea64cfefce5526538e1cdf191afb5022fb1cffabbd869c1

```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
9460790aa6707e5feea64cfefce5526538e1cdf191afb5022fb1cffabbd869c1
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.0
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
It is only a recommendation, not a dependency:

```
The following additional packages will be installed:
  catatonit conmon containernetworking-plugins containers-common containers-golang containers-image cri-o-runc crun dmsetup
  iptables libdevmapper1.02.1 libglib2.0-0 libglib2.0-data libgpgme11 libicu60 libip4tc0 libip6tc0 libiptc0 libmnl0
  libnetfilter-conntrack3 libnfnetlink0 libxml2 libxtables12 libyajl2 multiarch-support podman-plugins shared-mime-info uidmap
  varlink xdg-user-dirs
Suggested packages:
  kmod
Recommended packages:
  slirp4netns
The following NEW packages will be installed:
  catatonit conmon containernetworking-plugins containers-common containers-golang containers-image cri-o-runc crun dmsetup
  iptables libdevmapper1.02.1 libglib2.0-0 libglib2.0-data libgpgme11 libicu60 libip4tc0 libip6tc0 libiptc0 libmnl0
  libnetfilter-conntrack3 libnfnetlink0 libxml2 libxtables12 libyajl2 multiarch-support podman podman-plugins shared-mime-info
  uidmap varlink xdg-user-dirs
0 upgraded, 31 newly installed, 0 to remove and 0 not upgraded.
```

I suppose `podman run --network=none` works :-P
It is also missing `fuse-overlayfs`, see #8560. So basically, you should use `sudo podman` with this package.

@afbjorklund thank you for the answers :)

If slirp4netns is recommenced and Podman throws an error when I run a container, why is it not included in the opensuse repo :) ?

But, since slirp4netns not a dependency, can I then ignore this error? Is it safe / stable for me to run Podman like this in prodcution on Ubuntu 18.04?

I won't be using sudo to run the containers so it must work without sudo permissions.

If you run rootless podman, you _want_ to install both the `fuse-overlayfs` and `slirp4netns` packages.

It will "work" without them, but you get will get limited functionality (slow storage and limited networking)

However, it seems they are **not** available in the old distribution:

https://packages.ubuntu.com/search?keywords=fuse-overlayfs

https://packages.ubuntu.com/search?keywords=slirp4netns

So probably they will need to be backported, to Ubuntu 18.04 LTS
I don't see this as an upstream issue, can we close this issue?

@afbjorklund Thanks again for your answers.

I will close this ticket then and create an issue with the maintainer of the repository.

>  Is it safe / stable for me to run Podman like this in prodcution on Ubuntu 18.04?

It seems like you will need Ubuntu 20.04 then, only supported on e.g. RHEL 8

> I will close this ticket then and create an issue with the maintainer of the repository.

I thought this was the repository (for podman, and the needed dependencies)

But you are right, it's an issue with the packaging and not with podman itself.
I suppose you can install fuse-overlayfs and slirp4netns from source instead.

minikube start
😄  minikube v1.17.1 on Ubuntu 20.04
👎  Unable to pick a default driver. Here is what was considered, in preference order:
    ▪ vmware: Not installed: exec: "docker-machine-driver-vmware": executable file not found in $PATH
    ▪ docker: Not healthy: "docker version --format {{.Server.Os}}-{{.Server.Version}}" exit status 1: Got permission denied while trying to connect to the Docker daemon socket at unix:///var/run/docker.sock: Get http://%2Fvar%2Frun%2Fdocker.sock/v1.24/version: dial unix /var/run/docker.sock: connect: permission denied
    ▪ kvm2: Not installed: exec: "virsh": executable file not found in $PATH
    ▪ podman: Not installed: exec: "podman": executable file not found in $PATH
    ▪ virtualbox: Not installed: unable to find VBoxManage in $PATH

❌  Exiting due to DRV_NOT_DETECTED: No possible driver was detected. Try specifying --driver, or see https://minikube.sigs.k8s.io/docs/start/

Can any one help to troubleshoot this on a VMware workstation . How to install pod man?

sudo apt install podman
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
E: Unable to locate package podman

sudo apt-get -y install podman
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
E: Unable to locate package podman

> Can any one help to troubleshoot this on a VMware workstation . How to install pod man?

Please see https://podman.io/getting-started/installation#ubuntu
